executiveSummary.text: Navitas Semiconductor (NVTS) is now back in sync with the canonical DYOR HQ sheet after the metadata repair pass. The current stance is REDUCE with conviction 50/100, using $9.54 as the latest stored price reference where available.

businessModel.text: Navitas Semiconductor is exposed to semiconductor content growth, with valuation tied to design wins, adoption curves and margin progression rather than headline narratives alone.

recentCatalysts.text: Customer ramps, new platform wins, gross-margin direction and end-market recovery remain the main operating triggers.

thesisEvaluation.text: Base case: the core operating backdrop remains good enough to support the present reduce stance, but not strong enough to justify a looser risk view without better evidence. Bull case: execution and demand improve faster than expected. Bear case: operating or funding pressure worsens and compresses valuation further.

keyRisks.text: Customer concentration can create sharp earnings swings. Technology transitions may take longer than planned. Competition can compress pricing. Inventory corrections can hit near-term demand. Capital-market sentiment toward semis can overshoot fundamentals.

risks.risk: Customer concentration can create sharp earnings swings.
Technology transitions may take longer than planned.
Inventory corrections can hit near-term demand.
Capital-market sentiment toward semis can overshoot fundamentals.

whoShouldOwn.text: Own: Investors who understand semiconductor adoption cycles. Avoid: Investors seeking steady earnings and low volatility.

recommendation.text: REDUCE - Conviction: 50/100. This rebuilt page is intentionally concise, but it is now internally coherent and aligned to the corrected ticker and company metadata.

entryExit.text: Keep position sizing consistent with a reduce stance until fuller company-specific analysis is refreshed.
